description_text:
Position Overview

We are hiring a Machine Learning Engineer to help design, build, and deploy ML solutions. This role focuses on turning models into reliable systems and collaborating with engineering teams to operationalize ML.

Key Responsibilities
• Develop and train ML models with reproducible pipelines
• Build data preprocessing workflows and feature generation
• Deploy models to production and monitor performance
• Work with stakeholders to define success metrics and test outcomes
• Document experiments and contribute to best practices

Required Qualifications
• Degree in CS, Data Science, Engineering, or related field
• Strong Python, data handling (pandas), and ML fundamentals
• Familiarity with scikit-learn and basic model evaluation
• Understanding of software engineering basics (Git, testing, modular code)

Preferred Qualifications
• PyTorch or TensorFlow
• Experience with ML pipelines, Airflow, or workflow orchestration
• Exposure to CI/CD, Docker, and cloud services

Join the Artificial Intelligence Group at JHU/APL to support foundational AI research addressing national security challenges.

Your Role

Here’s what you will be doing:
• Support research programs targeting foundational AI advances with cross-mission impact.
• Work on improving alignment of AI agents for human-machine teaming and wargaming.
• Train multimodal foundation models on language and sensor data.
• Develop intelligent robots using agentic AI for complex, self-directed tasks.
• Assess, mitigate, and exploit vulnerabilities of frontier AI models.
• Conduct mission-driven test and evaluation (T&E) of novel AI research.
• Collaborate within the Artificial Intelligence Group in the Research and Exploratory Development Department (REDD) and the Intelligent Systems Center (ISC).
• Engage in interdisciplinary projects with opportunities for publication and high external visibility.

About You

The company is looking for candidates who meet the following qualifications:
• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or Engineering.
• Experience supporting AI/ML research on campus or during an internship.
• Proficiency in Python programming and experience using PyTorch.
• Eligibility for a DoD Top Secret security clearance (U.S. citizenship required).

Additional qualifications that are advantageous include:
• Maintaining an active portfolio of open-source software projects on GitHub.
• Experience applying AI/ML to real-world data such as full-motion video, satellite or medical imagery, electronic health records, or audio recordings.
• Experience building applications in Docker.
• Experience developing software for robotic systems using ROS/ROS2.
• Publication of work in scientific journals and/or conferences.

Compensation & Benefits
• Annual salary range: $85,000 to $165,000, prorated for part-time employees.
• Potential bonuses, sign-on bonuses, relocation benefits, locality allowances, or discretionary payments for exceptional performance.
• Comprehensive benefits package including retirement plans, paid time off, medical, dental, vision, life insurance, short-term and long-term disability, flexible spending accounts, education assistance, and training and development.
• Robust education assistance program and unparalleled retirement contributions.
• Healthy work/life balance and a vibrant, welcoming workplace culture.

Training & Development
• Opportunities to work on interdisciplinary projects with high external visibility.
• Chance to publish research work.
• Access to a facility with over 100 researchers specializing in AI, robotics, neuroscience, and complex systems.

How to Apply

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis.

This job may close before the stated closing date, you are encouraged to apply as soon as possible

Report this job

Vega is hiring! Fulltime job opportunity, Entry level BI Analyst. Must be located in New Jersey or live within a commutable distance to Montvale, NJ

The Business Intelligence Analyst (Entry Level) supports the Business Intelligence & Data team by understanding and writing data queries, building visualizations, and working with end users to obtain clear and concise report requirements. The candidate should have familiarity with data models/relationships and strong analytical skills with great attention to detail. Eagerness to learn the business is a must.

Key Responsibilities
• Assist in developing, maintaining, and enhancing dashboards and reports using BI tools
• Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources into BI tools and databases
• Perform data validation, reconciliation, and quality checks across multiple data sources
• Translate business requirements into technical reporting specifications
• Document data sources, transformations, metrics definitions, and report logic
• Collaborate with BI & Data team and business stakeholders to produce impactful reports
• Foundational knowledge of SQL (joins, aggregations)
• Experience working with structured datasets (tables, schemas, relationships)
• Familiarity with basic data warehousing and BI concepts
• Detail-oriented
• Ability to multi-task
• Strong communication skills with both technical and nontechnical users
• Bachelor s degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Information Systems, or a related field
• Hands-on experience with BI tools (experience with Qlik products is a plus)
• Familiarity with SQL Server
• Data Modeling Fundamentals

If you are looking for full time job opportunity and have the BI Skills - select "Apply Now" and a Vega Staffing specialist will reach out to you.

Emonics LLC is seeking a motivated Entry Level AI Engineer to join their New York team. The role involves assisting in developing AI-driven solutions, building models, and supporting data-driven product initiatives.

Responsibilities
• Develop and test AI/ML models under senior guidance
• Assist with data preprocessing and feature engineering
• Support deployment of AI models into production
• Collaborate with engineering and product teams
• Document processes and model performance

Skills
• Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI, Data Science, or related field
• Knowledge of Python, TensorFlow or PyTorch
• Understanding of machine learning fundamentals
• Strong problem-solving skills

Company Overview
• Emonics LLC connects you with the right talent at the right time. It was founded in 2016, and is headquartered in Jersey City, New Jersey, USA, with a workforce of 1001-5000 employees. Its website is https://www.emonics.com/.

Company H1B Sponsorship
• Emonics LLC has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 86 in 2025, 79 in 2024, 99 in 2023, 148 in 2022, 82 in 2021, 44 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Position Overview

Data Analyst - Entry Level (Remote). We are looking for the right people who want to innovate, achieve, grow and lead. We attract and retain the best talent by investing in our employees and empowering them to develop themselves and their careers. Experience the challenges, rewards and opportunity of working for one of the world's largest providers of products and services to the global energy industry.

Key Responsibilities
• Perform data engineering, data modeling and model deployment under general supervision.
• Analyze large scale complex business data (time series data, structured/unstructured) from various data sources and draw insights.
• Leverage common open-source Machine Learning/Deep Learning packages for identifying data patterns and/or building predictive models.
• Conduct statistical analysis to determine trends and significant data relationships.
• Keep up to date with the latest Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence advancements.
• Work with data engineers to design and construct data pipelines for reproducible analysis.
• Leverage cloud computing technologies like Microsoft Azure and distributed computing technologies like Apache Spark.
• Present results of analyses, including design of graphs, charts, tables, and other data visualizations.

Required Qualifications
• Industry experience in predictive modeling, data science and analysis.
• Knowledge of Machine Learning frameworks and packages, including Keras, TensorFlow, Scikit-Learn and cloud computing platforms like Azure.
• Experience handling terabyte size datasets, diving into data to discover hidden patterns and using data visualization tools.
• Experience writing code in Python, R, Scala, and distributed computing technologies like Spark.
• Demonstrated teamwork, strong communication skills, and collaboration in complex engineering projects.
• Completion of an undergraduate degree in STEM.

Preferred Qualifications
• Master's degree in STEM is preferred.
• Candidates with qualifications exceeding the minimum requirements will be considered for higher level roles.
